indicator_text: Whether lawmakers are required to file financial interest disclosures, and also whether they are complete and detailed
scoring_rule: 1-100 (in-law plus in-practice components, per article framing)
source_quote: Experienced journalists in each state undertook exhaustive research and reporting to score each of the questions, which ask, for example, whether lawmakers are required to file financial interest disclosures, and also whether they are complete and detailed. (papers/CPI_2015__sii_only_three_states.html)
notes: The ONE specific question paraphrased in the article. Treated as illustrative example, not verbatim wording. Bundles a de jure ('are required to file') and de facto ('complete and detailed') component — consistent with stated SII methodology of measuring both 'what the laws say' and 'how well they are enforced or implemented.'

indicator_text: Whether citizens actually receive responses to their requests swiftly and at reasonable cost
source_quote: Only one state — Missouri — received a perfect score on a question asking whether citizens actually receive responses to their requests swiftly and at reasonable cost. (papers/CPI_2015__sii_only_three_states.html)
notes: Second specific question paraphrased in the article. Pure de facto / in-practice indicator. Missouri was the only state to earn a perfect score on this question.

indicator_text: Whether governments are meeting open data principles (per-category sub-questions)
source_quote: The 2015 State Integrity Investigation included questions in each category asking whether governments are meeting open data principles. Almost universally, the answer was no. More than anything, these scores were responsible for dragging down the grades since the first round of the project. (papers/CPI_2015__sii_only_three_states.html)
notes: NEW in 2015 — open-data sub-questions were added to EACH of the 13 categories (so multiple distinct questions, not a single one). Global Integrity consulted the Sunlight Foundation when writing them. Primary driver of overall score declines vs 2012.
